toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns` attribute on `<Project>` — replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` — handled by the SDK
3. `Configuration` and `Platform` default properties — SDK defaults
4. `ProjectGuid` — not needed in SDK-style
5. `OutputType` Library — SDK default for class libraries


6. `RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName` matching project names — SDK defaults
7. `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `TargetFramework net472`
8. `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, and `Deterministic` — all SDK defaults
9. Debug/Release configuration blocks — SDK handles these
10. Explicit BCL assembly references and `<Compile Include` patterns — implicit in SDK-style projects
11. `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — auto-generated by SDK, with attributes moved to .csproj properties
12. `Microsoft.CSharp` reference — implicit

Building the modernized project structure with just the essential properties like target framework, description, company, copyright, and version, while letting the SDK handle everything else by default.

Now I'm checking which properties can be omitted because they match defaults or the project name — `RootNamespace`, `AssemblyName`, `AssemblyTitle`, and `AssemblyProduct` all match "LegacyApp", `ComVisible(false)` is already the default, the GUID isn't needed for SDK-style projects, and version information can be consolidated into the `Version` property. I'll clean up the project file, remove AssemblyInfo.cs, and delete the Properties directory.

data.content:
Here's a summary of the **15 legacy patterns removed** and what replaced them:

| Legacy Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` on `<Project>` | → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.Common.props` | SDK handles this |
| Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | SDK handles this |
| `Configuration` / `Platform` defaults | SDK defaults |
| `ProjectGuid` | Not needed in SDK-style |
| `OutputType` Library | SDK default for class libs |
| `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` matching project name | SDK infers from filename |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `TargetFramework net472` |
| `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (12 properties) | All SDK defaults |
| `<Reference>` for BCL assemblies (System, System.Core, etc.) | Implicit in SDK-style |
| `<Compile Include>` for every `.cs` file | Implicit globbing |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | **Deleted** — attributes moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) |

**Result:** 47 lines → 11 lines. The `.cs` source files are unchanged.
